fields.title: Booz Allen Hamilton Unlocks Talent Retention and Productivity Through Upskilling
fields.stat_2_text: of learners noted a positive impact on their productivity
fields.description:
In recent years, organizations in all industriesâ€”from health, to national security, to finance, to energyâ€”have realized the benefits of employing highlyskilled technical talent. Consequently, the demand for such talent has exploded, making it very difficult to recruit and retain these individuals. In fact, the demand for certain skill sets literally outweighs the supply.

Looking within Booz Allen, many employees with the necessary technical skillsets did not have the required clearance levels; and conversely, few with the required clearance level had the necessary technical skills. As a result, sold and funded positions often remained unfilled, sometimes for several months and even at the cost of the loss of the contract. The firm quickly realized that to position itself as a leading provider of solutions in technology, it would need to aggressively commit to reskilling and upskilling existing and newly hired employees.

The Learning and Development (L&D) team partnered with leaders across multiple business units and talent acquisition to determine where greatest needs existed across the workforce. The team analyzed volume of unfilled job requisitions to determine hard-to-fill jobs, interviewed leaders challenged with lack of qualified candidates, and assessed reasons why cleared applicants did not complete hiring process. Data showed the firm experienced talent shortages in four technical domains â€“ 1) Data Science, 2) Cyber Engineering, 3) Modern Software Development, and 4) Cloud Engineering.

fields.title: Capital One Accelerates Transformational Learning through Udemy Business
fields.stat_1_text: of learners rated Udemy as â€śvery helpfulâ€ť to their success
fields.stat_2_text: increase in retention for in-demand tech roles
fields.description:
With a large workforce of over 50,000 spread across several countries, Capital One uses a mix of online and instructor-led Learning and Development (L&D) programming. Associates receive a wide range of innovative learning resources and experiences, helping Capital One attract, develop, and retain the best talent and deliver on its business strategies.

One of the ways Capital One has invested in L&D is through Tech College. Developed in 2017, Tech College serves as the companyâ€™s internal tech-learning program and was designed as a way to keep engineers, product managers, and designers up-to-date on cutting-edge technology skills that could aid in the development of new products and applications.

Led by Capital Oneâ€™s Senior Director of Technology Learning, Eric Lawson, Tech College has expanded in size and scope since achieving the companyâ€™s cloud-based transformation. As Lawson explains, â€śWe use Tech College to help reskill and upskill our associates and ensure all new Capital One tech associates â€” both recent college graduates and experienced hires â€” are equipped with the right resources and training for success.â€ť

fields.title: Eventbrite Navigates Change Through Skill-Building and Leadership Development
fields.stat_1_text: increase in employee enrollments for professional development courses
fields.stat_2_text: revenue growth supported by a business model backed by learning
fields.description:
Since the company was founded, the core business focused on providing sales and services to support a diverse spectrum of live and in-person events such as musical concerts, holiday festivals, networking events and conferences, recreational, wellness and hobby classes, and several community and cultural events. The Covid 19 pandemic put a sudden halt to all in-person events and upended Eventbriteâ€™s business model.

With in-person events seeming implausible in the near future, the company needed to rapidly pivot to online events and services. This implied a shift from a sales and marketing focus to developing deep expertise as a product engineering company. Eventbrite needed to rapidly transform and rebuild its business model, restructure jobs and roles, and embark on a drastic skill-shift journey to support this change.

John Adcock, Eventbriteâ€™s Chief People Officer explains, â€śOur business came to a standstill when the pandemic struck. We needed to restructure and transform the business, and it required us to rethink our budget spend. We strongly believe that our investments in learning are indeed investments, and not costs. The Eventbrite team unanimously agreed to continue investing in skill development with Udemy rather than cutting back, reinforcing our focus on our people over processes.

fields.title: Toyota Tsusho Enhances its L&D Program to Improve Employee Outcomes
fields.description:
TTSPH covers eight countries across the South Pacific region. They are authorised distributors of Toyota, Hino, Yamaha, Massey Ferguson and other quality products, as well as used vehicles, car rentals and petrol stations, enhancing the business model of TTSPH across the South Pacific.

As a large group operating across culturally and geographically diverse countries, TTSPHâ€™s employee L&D program is broad and relied upon by over a thousand people. However, with primarily face-to-face classroom-based, instructor-led training, the company struggled to offer flexible alternatives to provide continuous learning despite travel restrictions.

In fact, a bi-annual employee survey of L&D requirements highlighted a significant need for ongoing staff development for employees, not just frontline and technical staff, without needing to attend the companyâ€™s regional training centers. In addition, TTSPH identified that its existing training program could only be accessed on request, providing slow, expensive and ad hoc training rather than a continuous on demand program.

TTSPH wanted to offer wideranging professional and personal L&D in a way that supported its response to COVID-19 remote working and without stretching training budgets. The company needed an online learning solution that could offer a broad range of content and support its dispersed employeesâ€™ nuanced objectives.
